extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won Paris sprint by finding gap on right while GROENEWEGEN went left in final 100m, taking third stage victory in debut Tour.
tactical_patterns: VIVIANI's leadout train (MARKOV-RICHEZE) positioned him fourth wheel but lost cohesion through Place de la Concorde corner.
EWAN positioned behind GROENEWEGEN then split opposite direction in final 100m when Dutchman committed left.
DECEUNINCK sprint train completely absent in finale despite ALAPHILIPPE doing early work for VIVIANI kilometers out.

rider_signals.VIVIANI Elio: negative: had dedicated leadout from national champions MARKOV and RICHEZE but went too wide on corner and nowhere in finale
rider_signals.HAGEN Edvald Boasson: negative: Dimension Data attempted daily stage hunt but failed conversion when leading inside final meters
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: committed to left side sprint line allowing EWAN to exploit right gap he created himself
rider_signals.EWING Caleb: positive: demonstrated superior positioning instinct by recognizing GROENEWEGEN's directional commitment created exploitable gap opposite side
